nodes.context: Ecuadorian organized crime syndicate involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and robbery.
Rival gang of Los Choneros in Ecuador, eventually exterminated.
Mexican drug cartel; Los Choneros function as its armed wing in Ecuador.

edges.evidence_quote: España was killed in a conflict between the Choneros and a rival gang by the name of the Queseros.
edges.context: Los Choneros and Los Queseros were in a violent conflict that led to the extermination of the Queseros.

edges.evidence_quote: The Choneros function as the armed wing of the Sinaloa Cartel within Ecuador.
edges.context: Los Choneros act as the armed wing of the Sinaloa Cartel in Ecuador.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Sinaloa Cartel, Clan del Golfo, Urías Rondón Front, Kompania Bello
edges.context: Los Choneros are allied with Clan del Golfo.

edges.context: Los Choneros are allied with Kompania Bello.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Los Lagartos, Los Lobos, Los Tiguerones, Los Chone Killers
edges.context: Los Choneros have Los Lagartos as rivals.

edges.context: Los Choneros have Los Lobos as rivals.

edges.evidence_quote: In Manabí, government crackdowns on the Choneros led to the emergence of violent splinter factions, including Los Pepes, a coalition of eight smaller gangs.
edges.context: Los Pepes emerged as a splinter faction from Los Choneros.

edges.evidence_quote: In the town of Durán, a key cocaine transshipment hub, turf wars between the Chone Killers (a splinter group of Los Choneros) and the Latin Kings have involved car-bombings targeting public officials.
edges.context: Los Chone Killers and Latin Kings are engaged in turf wars involving car-bombings.
